text.title: go-project-structure Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-project-structure as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Cyclic imports: package A imports B, package B imports A.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Go toolchain rejects cyclic imports at compile time. The fix is to extract shared types into a third package (e.g., internal/model), not to export more symbols upward.

failure-mode.description: Global state: var DB *sql.DB at package level.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Global DB makes parallel tests interfere, prevents multiple instances, and hides dependencies. Use constructor injection: NewUserRepository(db *sql.DB).

failure-mode.description: Everything in the main package: type User, type Order, func CreateUser, func HandleRequest all in package main.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Single-package projects become untestable and unmaintainable above ~500 lines. Separate by responsibility into internal/ sub-packages.
